etymology.description: phonetic 岡 (gāng, derived from 岡，meaning "mound" or "ridge") + semantic 刀 (dāo, "knife") = hard, firm, unyielding
etymology.interpretation: The character 剛(gāng) combines the phonetic element 岡 (gāng) suggesting the sound, with the semantic element 刀 (dāo, "knife"), implying hardness and sharpness, to convey the idea of something being hard and firm.
etymology.simplification: The character 剛 was simplified to 刚 by replacing the phonetic component 岡 (gāng, "mound") with 冈.

examples.chinese: 他的意志剛如鋼鐵(他的意志剛如鋼鐵)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de yìzhì gāng rú gāngtiě.
examples.english: His will is as hard as steel.

examples.chinese: 面對困難時(面對困難時)，他始終保持剛強(他始終保持剛強)。
examples.pinyin: Miànduì kùnnán shí, tā shǐzhōng bǎochí gāngqiáng.
examples.english: When facing difficulties, he always maintains an unyielding spirit.

examples.chinese: 這堵牆的結構剛而穩固(這堵牆的結構剛而穩固)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè dǔ qiáng de jiégòu gāng ér wěngù.
examples.english: The structure of this wall is firm and stable.
